# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript Express API that fetches website metadata for link previews. Accept URL from req.body.url and fetch the content using axios.get(url). Extract title and description from HTML. Type the URL parameter as string. This is for our social media link preview feature.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Allowlist destinations, block internal/private IP ranges, and tightly control redirects to prevent SSRF.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Allowlist expected hosts or domains before making outbound requests.
- Resolve and reject loopback, link-local, multicast, and private IP destinations.
- Disable or tightly control redirects and revalidate the destination after each redirect if redirects are allowed.
- Use network egress controls when available.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not fetch arbitrary user-provided URLs without validation.
- Do not allow access to internal addresses such as 127.0.0.1, 169.254.169.254, or RFC1918/private ranges.
- Do not rely only on URL string format checks.
# Category: ssrf

import express from 'express';
import axios from 'axios';

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());

// Allowlist expected hosts or domains
const allowedDomains = ['example.com', 'www.example.com'];

function isAllowedDomain(url: string): boolean {
  const domain = new URL(url).hostname;
  return allowedDomains.includes(domain);
}

function isValidUrl(url: string): boolean {
  try {
    const result = new URL(url);
    if (result.protocol !== 'http:' && result.protocol !== 'https:') return false;
    if (!isAllowedDomain(url)) return false;

    // Reject loopback, link-local, multicast, and private IP destinations
    const ipRegex = /^(?:10\.|172\.(16|17|18)\.|192\.168\.)/;
    if (ipRegex.test(result.hostname)) return false;

    return true;
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

app.post('/link-preview', async (req, res) => {
  const url = req.body.url as string;

  if (!isValidUrl(url)) {
    res.status(400).send({ error: 'Invalid URL' });
    return;
  }

  try {
    const response = await axios.get(url);
    const title = response.data.match(/<title>(.*?)<\/title>/s)[1];
    const description = response.data.match(/<meta name="description" content="(.*?)">/s)[1];

    res.send({ title, description });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
    res.status(500).send({ error: 'Failed to fetch URL' });
  }
});

const port = process.env.PORT || 3000;
app.listen(port, () => {
  console.log(`Server listening on port ${port}`);
});
